title: Dreamy Creamy Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A yeasty and creamy Algerian dish featuring hoisin sauce and flambe, made with ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly ingredients, served on a talavera plate.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay or ceramic pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the hoisin sauce, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the coconut cream and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Use a kitchen torch to flambe the dish, or simmer over low heat for an additional 10 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with fresh herbs, on a talavera plate.
